hypothesis: Incorporating explicit electronegativity difference tensors between shell 1 and shell 2 elements as an additional conditioning signal in the latent diffusion model will improve adsorption energy prediction accuracy and generation fidelity for multi-adsorbate systems, because the charge transfer dynamics governing ensemble and ligand effects are more directly encoded by inter-shell electronegativity gradients than by elemental identity alone, and this relationship will be empirically testable by comparing CompGen variants with and without electronegativity-difference conditioning on held-out HEA configurations spanning a broad range of adsorption energies.
